name: Chandan
sloka: स्वादु तिक्तं कषायं पीतं श्वेतं सुगन्धि तत् | शीतलं पित्तदाहघ्नं चन्दनं प्रोच्यते बुधैः ||
classification_of_gana: चरक: दाहप्रशमन, तृष्णानिग्रहण, वर्ण्य, कण्डूघ्न, विषघ्न

external_morphology.habitat: Forest of Karnataka and Tamil Nadu
details: Leaves: 4-6 cm long, elliptic lanceolate subacute, entire margin
Flower: Brownish purple, perianth is companulate

phytoconstituents: α-santalol, β-santene, santenol, santalenes

karma.dosha_karma: पित्तशामक, कफशामक (कटु विपाक, तिक्त रस). Agryakarma: on application removes bad odour, relieves burning sensation, acts as coolant.
karma.karma: तृषाहर, त्वक्प्रसादक, श्रमहर, दाहहर, ज्वरहर

rogaghnata: दाह, तृष्णा, रक्तपित्त, त्वकविकार, कुष्ठ, विसर्प, ज्वर, कृमी (Daha, Trishna, Raktapitta, Twak vikara, Kushtha, Visarpa, Jwara, Krumi)
amayika_prayoga: रक्तातिसार (Raktatisar) = चंदन चूर्ण + Sugar + Honey + Tandulodaka
रक्तार्ष (Raktarsha) = चंदन रस + शुंठी
पित्तजज्वर (Pittaja Jwara) = चंदन कषाय + Sugar + Honey
बहुमुत्र मेहा (Bahumutra Meha) = क्वाथ की अर्द्धन + चंदन
छर्दी (Chardi) = चंदन चूर्ण + Dhatri rasa
marga: Ext. application = लेप, तैल | Int. = क्वाथ
kalpana: Marma Gutika - Cardiac and liver disease
varieties: भावप्रकाश: श्वेत चंदन (Shweta), पीत चंदन (Peeta), रक्त चंदन (Rakta / Kuchandana)

name: Ela
sloka: सूक्ष्मा उपकुञ्चिका कुन्ती कोरङ्गी त्रुटिः | एला सूक्ष्मा कफश्वासकासअर्शोमूत्रकृच्छ्रहृत् | रसे तु कटुका शीता लघ्वी वातहरी मता ||

external_morphology.habitat: Mysore, Kung, Travancore, Cochin, Lanka and Burma; cultivated mainly in Western Ghats (South India)
details: Flowers: in panicle (produced directly from the root stock)
Seeds: 15-20 dark reddish brown to black hard seeds

phytoconstituents: Volatile oil 2-8%, seed oil - cineol, terpineol, limonene, terpinene

rasa_panchaka.rasa: Sthula Ela: Katu | Laghu Ela: Katu, Madhura
rasa_panchaka.guna: Sthula Ela: Laghu, Ruksha | Laghu Ela: Laghu, Sukshma, Snigdha
rasa_panchaka.virya: Sthula Ela: Ushna | Laghu Ela: Sheeta
rasa_panchaka.vipaka: Sthula Ela: Katu | Laghu Ela: Madhura

karma.dosha_karma: Tridoshahara. कफघ्न = Katu Rasa, Laghu Guna. पित्तघ्न = Madhur Vipaka, Sheeta Virya. वातघ्न = Madhur Anupada, Vishravana Guna, Madhur Vipaka.
karma.karma: रोचक, पाचक, कण्ठशोधन, हृद्य, वातशमन, छर्दीनिग्रहण, दुर्गन्धिनाशन, दिपन, तृष्णाहर, रक्त आर्शहर, मुत्रकृच्छ्रहर

rogaghnata: Trishna, अरुची (Aruchi), Agnimandhya, Adhma, Hridaydaubalya, Kasa, Swasa, Mutrakruchra, Daha, Arsha (Rakta)
amayika_prayoga: Ela churna + Aja dugdh (भावना) - अंजन in तिमिर (Timira)
Ela churna with मद्य (Madya) = Udavata
Ela churna + पिंपली + शुंठी + Jeevak with Dadim स्वरस = कण्ठगत रोग
Equal quantity of Ela and Pippali moola churna + Ghee = हृद्य रोग (Hridaya roga)
varieties: Type: Malabari - Mysori. Sukshma Ela (Elettaria cardamomum), Bruhat Ela (Amomum subulatum)
research_updates: Effect on B.P. (lowering) and anti-ulcer activity

name: Bhringraj
sloka: भृङ्गराजः कटुस्तिक्ष्णो रूक्षोष्णः कफवातनुत् | केश्यस्त्वच्यः कृमिश्वासकासशोथआमपाण्डुनुत् || त्वच्या रसायनी बाल्याः कुष्ठनेत्र शिरो अतिनुत् ||

external_morphology.habitat: India, marshy places, grows rapidly in rainy season
details: Leaf: Ramal, cauline, simple opposite elliptic-lanceolate, reticulate venation

phytoconstituents: Wedololactone, demethylwedololactone, ecliptal, β-amyrin, stigmasterol

karma.dosha_karma: Kaphahara = Katu, Tikta Rasa; Vataghna = Ushna Veerya, Laghu, Ruksha Guna
karma.karma: केश्य, त्वच्य, केशरञ्जन, यकृतोत्तेजक, नेत्र्य, रसायन, बल्य, शिरःशूलहर, विषघ्न

rogaghnata: Kasa, Swasa, Ama, Pandu, Kustha, Shirogata, Chakshuroga, Yakrutroga, Pliha vruddhi
amayika_prayoga: Kansis bhasma + Bhringaraj swaras = Kesharanjan
Goat milk + Bhringaraj swaras (Nasya) = Headache (Suryavarta)
Castor oil + Bhringaraj swaras (internally) = Intestinal worm infection
Bhringaraj kalka (external application) = Swollen area, abscess, throat infection, asthma, cough, cold, elephantiasis
Bhringaraj churna + Honey = Throat infection, asthma, cough, cold
research_updates: Alcoholic extract of Eclipta alba shows in-vitro antioxidant and anticancer activity without exhibiting toxicological effects

name: Bilwa
sloka: तिक्तं शाण्डिल्यशैलूषो मालूरश्रीफलादपि | श्रीफलस्तुवरस्तिक्तः ग्राही रूक्षोअग्निदीपनः | वातश्लेष्महरो बल्यो मधुरूष्णश्च पाचकः ||

synonyms.meaning: Fruit of Bilwa is having aroma

external_morphology.habitat: Found throughout India
details: Leaf: Palmately compound, trifoliate, petiolate, alternate, net veined
Flowers: Pedicellate, bisexual, sweet scented, petals white colour

phytoconstituents: Marmelosin, essential oils, tannic acid, rutin, aegeline, fatty acid

rasa_panchaka.rasa: Apakwa Phala: Kashaya, Tikta | Pakwa Phala: Madhura | Mula: Kashaya, Tikta
rasa_panchaka.guna: Apakwa Phala: Laghu, Snigdha | Pakwa Phala: Guru | Mula: Laghu, Ruksha
rasa_panchaka.virya: Apakwa Phala: Ushna | Pakwa Phala: Sheeta | Mula: Ushna
rasa_panchaka.vipaka: Apakwa Phala: Katu | Pakwa Phala: Madhura | Mula: Katu

karma.karma: Root: Shothahara, Shulahara | Stem: Sangrahi, Pramehaghna | Apakwa Phala: Deepan, Pachan, Grahi, Hrudya | Pakwa Phala: Vishtambhi | Pushpa: Atisarahara, Chardighna

rogaghnata: Root: Shotha, Shula | Leaf: Prameha | Stem: Kasa, Archi | Apakwa Phala: Atisara | Flowers: Trishna, Chardi
amayika_prayoga: Atisara = Apakwa Phala
Arshas (Piles) = Sitz bath with warm decoction of Bilwa
Medoroga = Kwatha of Brihatpanchamoola
Raktatisara = Bilwa Phala + Jaggery + Honey + Oil

name: Gambhari
sloka: काश्मरी तुवरा तिक्ता वीर्योष्णा मधुरा गुरुः | दीपनी पाचिनी मेध्या शोथदोषत्रयापहा | दाह तृष्णाऽरुचि शूल कर्षी विबन्ध ज्वरापहा | तस्याः फलं बृहणं वृष्यं गुरु केश्यं रसायनम् ||

external_morphology.habitat: Himalaya, Deccan Peninsula, Andaman
details: Habit: Large deciduous tree, 20 m height
Leaves: Large, tomentose underneath, cordate-ovate, acuminate

phytoconstituents: Gmelanol, butyric acid, tartaric acid, gcliosone

rasa_panchaka.rasa: Root: Tikta, Kashaya, Madhura | Fruit: Madhura

karma.dosha_karma: त्रिदोषशामक (Tridoshashamak). Root: Ushna Virya = Deepan; Ushna Virya + Balya = Anuloman; Ushna + Guru + Madhura Anupaka = Vataja Rasa.
karma.karma: Root bark: Shodhan (Garbhashaya Shotha), Vishaghna (snake and scorpion bite, internal and external use) | Fruit: Sheeta - Pittaghna, Trishnashamak; Madhura Rasa/Vipaka/Virya - Sandhaniya (hairline fracture etc), Balya - Rasayana, Urakshata | Ripe fruit: Sheeta - Raktapittaghna, Pittaja Jwara - Daha, Trishna; Madhura Vipaka, Sheeta, Guru, Snigdha - Garbhasthapana, Stanyajanan, Vrishya; Balya - Samanya Daurbalya; Mutrajanan and Mutrakruchra | Leaves: Mutrajanan (Mutrakruchra); Snigdha, Sheeta - Jwara, Shiroshoola and Daha (Patra Lepa) | General Karma: Deepan, Pachan, Anuloman, Shothahara, Balya, Mutrajanan, Stanyajanan, Shamana

rogaghnata: Agnimandhya, Atisara, Vibandha, Shotha, Urahkshata, Daurbalya, Mutrakruchra, Vatarakta, Gatikroga, Raktapitta
amayika_prayoga: Patha Swaras + Mishri = Mutrakruchra
Patra Lepa - Shirahshoola | Puyameha
Phala Yusha + Sugar - Raktatisara = Bastishotha
Flower and Fruit kashaya + Shamali - useful as diet
Gambhari Fruit + Yashtimadhu Siddha Ghrita + Ghrita = Garbhashushkta
Gambhari Phala Majja Kwatha + Mishri = Trishna, Daha, Jwara
Majja Kwatha + Madhu = Raktapitta
Ripe fruit powder + Milk = Sheetapitta
varieties: Substitute: Gambhari fruit used as substitute for Grapes / Raisins (भावप्रकाश)

name: Arjuna
sloka: ककुभो शीतलो हृद्यः क्षतक्षयविषापहा | मेदोलमेहत्वग्दोषान् हन्ति ज्वरः कफापहकृत ||
classification_of_gana: चरक: उदर्दप्रशमन, कषायस्कन्ध (Allergic skin condition, ringworm infection)

external_morphology.habitat: A large tree
details: Bark: Smooth, grey; sapwood reddish white; heartwood brown
Leaves: Subopposite, 10-15 cm long, oblong / elliptic-oblong
Inflorescence: Short axillary spikes, terminal panicle

phytoconstituents: Bark: calcium and magnesium salts, flavonoids (arjunic acid, tannins, arjunaglucosides)

karma.dosha_karma: विशेष: कफहर - कटु विपाक; पित्तहर - शीत वीर्य; हृद्य (Kaphahara - Katu Vipaka; Pittahara - Sheeta Virya; Hrudya)
karma.karma: हृद्य, वर्णहर, भग्नसंधानकर (Hrudroga, Pitta-Kapha Vrana) | Raktapittahara - bleeding disorders | Shramahara, Trishnahara

rogaghnata: Hrudroga, Kshata, Prameha, Bhagna, Asrujita, Pitta-Kapha Vrana
amayika_prayoga: Arjuna Kwatha + Churna = Osteoporosis
Arjuna + Godhuma + Oil, Ghee, Guda, Milk = Cardiac disease, Osteoporosis
Kwatha of Arjuna = Shukrameha
Kalka of Arjuna (Swaras) + Honey = Twakroga
Arjuna Churna + Laksha + Guggul + Ghee = Bhagna (internal use)
varieties: Types: White - Arjuna; Black - Kukubha. Adulteration: Terminalia alata, Terminalia tomentosa

name: Haridra
sloka: हरिद्रा कटुका तिक्ता रूक्षोष्णा कफपित्तनुत् | वर्ण्या त्वग्दोषमेहास्त्रशोथपाण्डुव्रणापहा ||
classification_of_gana: चरक: लेखनीय, कुष्ठघ्न, कण्डूघ्न, विषघ्न, तिक्तस्कन्ध, शिरोविरेचन

details: Named निशा / रजनी owing to its yellow coloured rhizome

phytoconstituents: 5% volatile oil, curcuminoids (curcumin). Rhizome volatile oil contains mono and sesquiterpenes such as α-pinene, phellandrene, camphor, camphene, zingiberene, curcumenes

karma.karma: वर्ण्य, त्वच्य, प्रमेहहर, शोथहर, व्रणहर, कण्डूघ्न, विषघ्न, कुष्ठघ्न, केशिनी, रुच्य

rogaghnata: शीतपित्त, कण्डू, कामला, श्वास, कुष्ठ, तिमिर, Naktandya, पाण्डु, प्रमेह, Dadru Kustha (ringworm infestation), Shlipada (Filariasis), वातरक्त (Gout), Panduroga (Anaemia), Kaphaja Trishna
amayika_prayoga: प्रमेह (Prameha) = Haridra powder + Honey + Amlaki, mixed in equal quantity
Dadru Kustha (ringworm infestation) = Haridra powder + cow's urine + jaggery
वातरक्त (Gout) = Kwatha of Haridra + Amrita taken with honey (kapha-dominant Vatarakta)
Panduroga (Anaemia) = Haridra + Triphala churna + Ghee and Honey
Kaphaja Trishna = Water with Haridra mixed with Honey and Sugar
varieties: आ. प्र.: Haridra, Amrahaidra, Vanaharidra, Daruharidra. डल्हण: Pinda Haridra, Daruharidra
research_updates: Antitumor activity in human colon cancer

name: Daruharidra
sloka: दार्वी दारुहरिद्रा स्यात् कटुष्णा व्रणमेहनुत् | कर्णनेत्रमुखोद्भूतां रुजं कण्डूं च नाशयेत् ||

details: Grows to a height of 2-3 metres
Twigs whitish to pale yellowish-brown in colour
Bark pale brown from outside and deep yellow from inside, deeply furrowed, rough
Leaves obovate or elliptic, entire or spinous toothed

phytoconstituents: Berberine, quaternary ammonium salt of isoquinoline alkaloid

karma.karma: Shothahara, Kanda Kushthahara, Visarpahara, Vishahara, Kaphahara, Abhishyandihara

rogaghnata: Shotha, Kanda Kushtha, Visarpa, Visha, Abhishyanda, Skin diseases (Kustha)
amayika_prayoga: Skin diseases (Kustha) - Rasanjana with Gomutra administered for treatment of Kustha

name: Haritaki
sloka: हरितकी पञ्चरसा अलवणा तुवरा परम | रुक्षा उष्ण दीपनी मेध्या स्वादुपाक रसायनी || चर्तिता वर्धयत्यग्निं पेषिता मलाशोधिनी | स्विन्ना संग्राहिणी पथ्या भृष्टा प्रोक्ता त्रिदोषनुत् ||
classification_of_gana: चरक: प्रजास्थापन, वयस्थापन, कुष्ठघ्न, कासघ्न, अर्शोघ्न

details: Large sized deciduous tree, height up to 80 ft

phytoconstituents: Important source of tannin - contains up to 32% tannin; on hydrolysis yields chebulic acid, d-galloyl glucose, sorbitol, chebulic acid, gallic acid

karma.karma: स्रोतोविशोधनी, रसायन, मेध्या, दीपनी, पाचिनी, लघु, संग्राहिणी, त्रिदोषनुत्

rogaghnata: आनाह, आध्मान, अश्मरी, विबन्ध, श्वास, कास, अरुची, कुष्ठ, मेदरोग, शोथ, केश्य, हृद्य, पाण्डु, अग्निमांद्य; used as a Rasayana
amayika_prayoga: Parinama Shula (duodenal ulcer) - Haritaki taken with jaggery and sugar
वातव्याधि (Vatavyadhi) = Haritaki + cow's urine
अतिस्वेद (Atisweda) = Paste of Haritaki
विबन्ध (Constipation) = Haritaki + Jaggery
छर्दी (Vomiting) = Haritaki + Honey
अर्श (Piles) = Haritaki + Cow's urine + Jaggery
वातरक्त (Vatarakta) = Haritaki churna + Jaggery
प्रमेह (Prameha) = हरीतकी + हरिद्रा + शर्करा
varieties: Types: विजया, रोहिणी, पूतना, अमृता, अभया, जीवन्ती, चेतकी. Ritu Haritaki (seasonal Anupana to achieve Rasayana Karma): Shishira (late winter) - Pippali; Vasanta (spring) - Madhu; Grishma (summer) - Guda; Varsha (rainy season) - Saindhava Lavana; Sharad (Autumn) - Sharkara; Hemanta (early winter) - Shunthi
research_updates: Anti-inflammatory, immunomodulatory, free radical scavenging and healing properties
Controls blood glucose and manages Type 2 diabetes

name: Hingu
sloka: हिङ्गुष्णं पाचनं रुच्यं तीक्ष्णं वातकफापहम् । शूलगुल्मोदरा नाहकृमिघ्नं पित्तवर्धनम् ॥ भा. प्र

external_morphology.habitat: Native to Iran and Afghanistan
details: Leaves: pubescent when young, ovate shape

phytoconstituents: Resin, gum, volatile oil

karma.karma: पाचन, दिपन, रुच्य, कृमिघ्न, शूलहर

rogaghnata: अग्निमांध्य, शूल, गुल्म, कृमि, कास, श्वास
amayika_prayoga: मदात्यय = Hingu & maricha given with wine
कर्णशूल = hingvadi taila

name: Jambu
sloka: जम्बू कषायमधुरा श्रमपित्तदाहकण्ठार्तिशोषशमनी कृमिदोषहारिणी

external_morphology.habitat: Evergreen tree throughout India

phytoconstituents: β-sitosterol, β-D-glucoside

karma.karma: संग्राही, दाहशमन, प्रमेहहर, ज्वरहर

rogaghnata: प्रमेह, अतिसार, अरुचि, दाह, कृमि
amayika_prayoga: रक्तपित्त = Jambu + Amra + Arjuna
मधुमेह = Jambu seeds powder daily

name: Jatamansi
sloka: जटामांसी तु तुवरा शीतला क्रान्तिकारका बल्या कदर्वी स्वादुतिक्ता

external_morphology.habitat: Himalayan region, alpine meadows
details: Flower: Rosy, pale pink, blue coloured

phytoconstituents: Jatamansic acid, calarene, Jatamansin

karma.karma: मेध्य, संज्ञास्थापन, दाहहर, विषघ्न

rogaghnata: मानसिक विकार, ज्वर, दाह, त्वक विकार
amayika_prayoga: कुष्ठ = paste from mansi with urine and alkali
केशवर्धनार्थ = Paste with milk and honey

name: Jyotishmati
sloka: ज्योतिष्मती कटुतिक्तोष्णा सरा कफसमीरजित् । अत्युष्णा वामनी तीक्ष्णा वह्निबुद्धिविवर्द्धिनी ॥ भा. प्र

external_morphology.habitat: Climber found in forests
details: Bark: pale brown, rough with shallow cracks
Seeds: completely enclosed in an orange-red aril

phytoconstituents: seed = 52% oil (thick reddish yellow), Tanin, celastin, celapanine, celastrol

karma.karma: मेध्य, बुद्धिवर्धन, स्रंसिनी, वामक, वर्ण्य, दिपन, श्वेदजनन

rogaghnata: manasika vikara, vrana, visarpa, pandu, vairanya, agnimandya
amayika_prayoga: sidhma (white spotted leprosy) = oil processed with water, mixed with jatamansi & decanted 7 times
udara (Ascitis) = Jyotishmati oil mixed with milk & swarjika alkali with Hingu
kalpana: Jyotishmati taila - manasika vikara, medhya karma

name: Kanchanara
sloka: काञ्चनारो हिमो ग्राही तुवरः श्लेष्मपित्तहृत् । गण्डगण्डार्बुदग्रन्थिकुष्ठव्रणकफापहः ॥ भा. प्र

external_morphology.habitat: Tropical regions

phytoconstituents: stem bark = β-sitosterol, stigmasterol
rogaghnata: vranavikara, Atisara, Apachi, gandamala, krimi, kushtha, shwasa
amayika_prayoga: Gandamala (Goitre) = kwatha of kanchanara with shunthi powder
masurika (pox) = decoction with swarna makshika powder

name: Kantakari
sloka: कण्टकारी रसा तिक्ता कटुका दीपिनी लघुः । केशापथ्या चानिलामकासश्वासज्वरापहा ॥ भा. प्र.

external_morphology.habitat: Wild herb in tropical regions
details: leaves: alternate, simple reticulate unicostate venation

phytoconstituents: alkaloid solasoline, scopolin, esculin
rogaghnata: शोष, श्वास, कास, ज्वर, कण्ठरोग, अग्निमांध्य, अजीर्ण, हिक्का, हृद्रोग, कुष्ठ, अश्मरी, पीनस, पार्श्वशूल

name: Kapikacchu
sloka: कपिकच्छूर्वृष्या स्वगुप्ता बृंहणी गुरुः । तिक्ता वातहरी बल्या स्निग्धा पित्तलास्त्रकृत् ॥ भा. प्र.

external_morphology.habitat: Tropical regions

phytoconstituents: mucuadine, mucuadinine, l-dopa, serotonin

karma.karma: वृष्य, बृंहण, बल्य, व्रणनाशक, वातशामक

rogaghnata: klaibya, Daurbalya, Dushtavrana, krisha
amayika_prayoga: vajikarana (Aphrodisiac) = powder mixed with godhuma & processed with milk
vatavyadhi-Apabahuka (paralysis) = seed juice gives more strength
research_updates: enhanced production of L-dopa in cell-cultures

name: Karkatashringi
sloka: शृंगी कषायतिक्तोष्णा कफवातज्वरप्रणूत । श्वासकासकफोत्क्लेशहिक्काक्षयविनाशिनी ॥

external_morphology.habitat: Trees in forests

phytoconstituents: tannin, resin, essential oil - camphene, α-pinene, α-terpineol

karma.karma: cheyan, grahi, chardighni

rogaghnata: Karkatashrungi = shwasa, kasa, Hikka, Aruchi, Trishna, chardi, jwaraghna, Atisara. Ajashrungi = shopha, Trishna, chardi, chakshu vikar, swasa, Hridrog, visha, kushtha
amayika_prayoga: shwasa & Hikka = gruel prepared with Karkatashrungi
kasa = powder licked with taila
kapha chardi = Karkat. churna mixed with musta churna given with honey
vajikarana = kalika preparation from milk

name: Kutaki
sloka: कट्वी तु कटुका पाके तिक्ता रूक्षा हिमा लघुः । भेदनी दीपनी हृद्या कफपित्तज्वरापहा ॥ प्रसूतस्तन्यश्वासकासदाहकृच्छ्रविनाशिनी ॥

external_morphology.habitat: Himalayan regions

phytoconstituents: Picroside-I, II, kutkin, kutkoside, kurrin, kuttiol

karma.karma: Rechan, bhedan, Hrudya, jwaraghna, pramehahar, swasahar, kasahar, dahahar, kushthaghna, स्तन्यशोधन

rogaghnata: kamala, mehavikara, vibandha, Hridroga, agnimandya, jwara, vishajwara, prameha, swasa, kasa, daha, kushtha, krimi, arsha, raktavikara
amayika_prayoga: stanya shuddhi = decoction prepared from katuki
Hridroga = kalka from katuki with madhuka given with sugar-water

name: Khadira
sloka: खदिरः शीतलो दन्त्यः कषायः कण्डूकासकृमिप्रणूत । तिक्तः कषायो मेदोघ्नः पित्तश्लेष्महरः लघुः । श्वित्रशोथविषारुचिपाण्डुकुष्ठव्रणान् हरेत् ॥ भा. प्र
phytoconstituents: 10% acacatechin, quercitin, gum quercitrin

karma.general: कुष्ठघ्न, कण्डूघ्न, दन्त्य, केश्य, रक्तशोधन, मेदोहर, ज्वरघ्न, शोथहर, पाचन

rogaghnata: कुष्ठ, दन्तरोग, कण्डू, कास, ज्वर, अरुचि, कृमि, शोथ, श्वित्र, प्रमेह, मेदरोग
amayika_prayoga: Kushtha = Khadira for drinking, bathing
shwitra (vitiligo) = haridra decoction
kasa = powder of heartwood mixed with wine or water

name: Dhanyaka
sloka: धान्यकं तुवरं स्निग्धं मूत्रलं दीपनं लघु | तिक्तं कटूष्णवीर्यं च तृष्णादाहवमिप्रणुत || ज्वरघ्नं शूलहृद्ग्राहि स्वादुपाकि त्रिदोषनुत् | आर्द्रन्तु तद्गुणं स्वादु विशेषाच्छीतलनाशि तत् ||

external_morphology.habit: Annual Herb with glabrous, aromatic white Flowers

phytoconstituents: coriandrinol, citronellol, coriandrin, carotene eugenol

karma.general: अग्रुष्य, मुत्रलमागं, अग्नीदीपक, आमपाचक, रोचक, ग्राही

rogaghnata: तृष्णा, दाह, छर्दी, शिरःशूल, कास, श्वास, हिक्का, ज्वर, कुष्ठघ्न, मूत्रकृच्छ्र
amayika_prayoga: Headache & burning = paste of raw coriander fruit
दाह & तृष्णा from fever = dhanyaka हिम with mishri & madhu
Jirna pratishyaya = Dhanyaka Fant with mishri
shotha, shula = dhanyaka लेप
नासागत रक्तस्त्राव (Epistaxis) = Nasya of dhanyaka leaves juice
हिक्का = धान्यक चूर्ण with शर्करा
research_updates: cholesterol lowering properties in coriander seeds

name: Japa
sloka: जपा संग्राहिणी केश्या त्रिसन्ध्या कफवातजित् ॥ भा. प्र.

external_morphology.bark: light Grey, easy to peel & smooth
external_morphology.leaves: ovate, simple, spirally arranged (8-10.5 cm long) margin - lobed/toothed
external_morphology.flowers: Bisexual, large, Bellshaped, stalked, petals joined at base

phytoconstituents: malvate, cyclopropanids, β-sistosterol, Amino acid - aspartic acid & asparagin

karma.general: केशरञ्जन (hair coloring), केशवर्धन (hair growth), sthambhan, mutrasangrahan

rogaghnata: पालिथ्य (premature greying), खालित्य (hair fall), Raktapradara, Phirang vran, mutrasangrahan
amayika_prayoga: केशरंजन = Japa Flowers + सुराशन Flowers in sheep milk
Alopecia = Japa Flowers with गोमूत्र on scalp
Raktapradara = Tender Flower paste with cow's milk
Phirang vran = wash with kwatha of Japa leaves
contraceptives = Flowers & Tender buds
Nourishment of womb = kwath of root & leaf

name: Chitrak
sloka: चित्रकः कटुकः पाके वह्निकृत् पाचनो लघुः । रुक्षोष्णो ग्रहणीकुष्ठ शोथ - अर्शः कृमि-कासजित् । वात श्लेष्महरो ग्राही वातार्शः श्लेष्मपित्तहृत् ॥ भा. प्र.

synonyms.term: अग्नि / वह्नि / ज्योति / दहन / ज्वाला / निर्दहन

classification_of_gana: चरक: लेखनीय, भेदनीय, तृप्तिघ्न, अर्शोघ्न, शूलप्रशमन
सुश्रुत: पिप्पल्यादि, वचादि, मुस्तादि, आमलक्यादि, वरुणादि

external_morphology.habitat: Tropical regions, widely distributed in India, Sri Lanka
details: Habit: Perennial herb or undershrub, climbing or erect
Leaves: Simple, alternate, ovate-lanceolate, 5-9 cm long
Flowers: White, in terminal spikes, heterostylous
Seeds: Reddish brown to dark brown

phytoconstituents: Plumbagin (major naphthoquinone), sitosterol, tannins, enzymes invertin & plumbagolic acid

karma.dosha_karma: वात-श्लेष्महर (Vata-Kaphahara). कटुक पाक = Kaphahara. उष्ण वीर्य, रूक्ष गुण = Vatahara.
karma.karma: वह्निकृत् (Deepana), पाचन (Pachana), ग्राही (Grahi - absorbent), लेखन (Lekhana), भेदन (Bhedana)

doshaghnata: वातश्लेष्महर, ग्राही वातार्शः श्लेष्मपित्तहृत् (Vata-Shleshmehar, Grahi, Vata-Arshas-Shleshma-Pittahrit)
rogaghnata: ग्रहणी (Grahanī), कुष्ठ (Kushtha), शोथ (Shotha), अर्श (Arsha), कृमि (Krumi), कास (Kasa), अतिसार (Atisara)
amayika_prayoga: रसायन (Rasayana) = Chitrak + milk / ghee & honey
कुष्ठ (Leucoderma) = Chitrak + cow urine churna
अर्श (Haemorrhoids) = Sudha Chitra root rubbed with cow's ghee - fine paste (External use)
श्वित्र (Vitiligo) = Chitrak + milk (cooked) - external application of this milk
अतिसार (Atisara/Diarrhoea) = Chitrak + Buttermilk (तक्र) churna
varieties: Varieties - श्वेता (Shweta), रक्त (Rakta), नीला (Neela). Shodhana: Red variety cut in small pieces → immersed in 3-4% lime water for 3hrs → taken out & immersed in fresh lime water → repeat 3 times
research_updates: Plumbagin shows potent anticancer activity against various cancer cell lines
Wound healing activity of Plumbago zeylanica root extract

name: Bhallataka
sloka: भल्लातकः कषायोष्णः शुक्रलो मधूरो लघुः । वातश्लेष्मोदरानाह कुष्ठअर्शो गृहणीगदान् । हन्ति तैलफलवत् श्वित्रवह्निमन्दयकृदगदान् ॥ भा. प्र.

external_morphology.habitat: Sub-Himalayan region, tropical parts of India, Central India
details: Habit: Moderate-sized deciduous tree, exudating dark juice
Leaves: Oblong-obovate, rounded at apex, 30-60 cm long
Flowers: Greenish-yellow coloured, fasciculate (in bundles), compound panicles
Fruits: Drupes, smooth, shining, purplish-black when ripe, cup-orange red
Seeds: Smooth kernel inside hard shell

phytoconstituents: Nuts = bioflavonoids, tetrahydromentoflavone. Fruits = Nicotinic Acid (B3), riboflavin (B2), thiamine (B1), essential amino acids

karma.dosha_karma: कफ-वात हर (Kapha-Vata hara). उष्ण वीर्य, कटु-तिक्त-कषाय रस → Vata-Kaphahara
karma.karma: मेध्य (Medhya), वृष्य (Vrishya), विषम (Vishama), भेदन (Bhedana), दीपन (Deepana), वातश्लेष्मोदरानाहहर

rogaghnata: क्रिमीहारी, उरुस्तम्भ, अर्श (Arsha), कुष्ठ (Kushtha), कृमी, गुल्म (Gulma), शूल (Shoola), वातव्याधी (Vatavyadhi), श्वित्र (Shwitra), आनाह (Anaha), ग्रहणी (Grahanī)
amayika_prayoga: उरुस्तम्भ, गर्भ कुष्ठ = B + Pippali + Pippalimula paste with honey
सर्व कुष्ठ = B + Abhaya + Vidanga kwatha or only B. oil
इन्द्रलुप्त (Alopecia) = B. rasa + madhu lep on head
अर्श (Arsha) = B + taila (equal qty.) consumed well
कफज गुल्म = B. kalka + kwatha siddha ghrita given with sitka
प्लीहोदर = B + Abhaya + Jeeraka with sitka orally
रसायन (Rasayana) = B. oil with milk for 1 month
B. majjana = Dental Shoola (toothache)
B. lavana = Agnimandya, Gulma, Adhmana, Yakrita vikara
varieties: Varieties - भल्लातक, Nadi Bhallataka. Purification: Bhallatakas preserved in brick powder for 7-12 days
research_updates: Wound healing - stem bark extract of Semecarpus anacardium

name: Amalaki
sloka: हन्ति वातं तदम्लत्वात् पित्तं माधुर्यशैत्यतः । कफं रुक्षकषायत्वात् फलं धात्र्यास्त्रिदोषजित् ॥ भा. प्र.

external_morphology.habitat: Tropical parts of India, Nepal, Sri Lanka. Deciduous forests, also cultivated.
details: Habit: Medium-sized tree, growing up to 8-18 m in height
Leaves: Simple, subsessile, light green, pinnate
Fruit: Spherical, light greenish-yellow, smooth, shining with 6 vertical stripes
Bark: Light greyish-brown, exfoliating in scales

phytoconstituents: Fruit juice contains phenolic constituents: gallic acid, l-malic acid, 2-O-gallate, mucic acid 2-O-gallate. Rich in Vitamin C (ascorbic acid), tannins, pectin

rasa_panchaka.rasa: पंचरस (all 5 tastes except लवण) - आम्ल प्रधान (predominantly sour)

karma.dosha_karma: त्रिदोषघ्न (Tridoshaghna): वातघ्न (amla rasa), पित्तघ्न (madhura vipaka, sheeta virya), कफघ्न (ruksha kashaya)
karma.karma: रसायन, चक्षुष्य, वृष्य, दाहहर, वलीपलितनाशन, आमघ्न, कण्ठ्य, मेदहर, भग्नसंधानकृत्, केश्य, कुष्ठघ्न

rogaghnata: रसायन, प्रमेह, ज्वर, रक्तपित्त, पाण्डुरोग, कामला, शुक्रदौर्बल्य, दाह, छर्दी, भ्रम, शोथ, केश्य विकार
amayika_prayoga: प्रमेह (Prameha) = Fresh Amalaki juice mixed with Haridra
ज्वर (Jwara) = Amalaki juice fried with ghee
पाण्डु (Pandu) = Amalaki churna mixed with Honey
कामला (Kamala) = Amalaki juice + grapes
रक्तपित्त (Raktapitta) = Amalaki churna + more quantity madhu; sharkara given as virechan
हिक्का (Hikka) = Amalaki juice + Kapittha mixed with Pippali churna + madhu
कास (Kasa) = Amalaki churna boiled with milk & taken with ghee
वातरक्त (Vatarakta / Gout Arthritis) = Kashaya of Amalaki + Haridra
varieties: Not mentioned separately in classical texts
research_updates: Inhibits UVB-induced photo-aging in human skin fibroblast
usefulPartAttribution: Wikimedia Commons - Phyllanthus emblica fruit (Dinesh Valke)

name: Jatiphala
sloka: जातीफलं रसे तिक्तं तीक्ष्णोष्णं रोचनं लघु । कटुकं दीपनं ग्राहि स्वर्यं श्लेष्मानिलापहम् । निहन्ति मुखवैशद्यं मलमूत्रविबद्धताः ॥ भा. प्र

external_morphology.habitat: Native to Maluku Islands (Indonesia), cultivated in tropical regions including Kerala, India
details: Habit: Tree with slender branches, evergreen, up to 10-15 m
Flowers: 6 mm long, pale yellow, fragrant
Fruit: Ovoid, pendulous, yellow when ripe
Seed: Nutmeg (Jatiphala), surrounded by aril (Jatipatri / Mace)

phytoconstituents: Volatile oil containing myristicin, myristic acid, d-pinene, d-camphene, safrole, elemicin

karma.general: जातीफल: ग्राहि, दीपन, रोचन, स्वर्य, कासहर, श्वासहर, वृष्य, हृद्य, कृमिघ्न, शूलहर, चक्षुष्य | जातीपत्री: रुचिकर, वृष्य, कासहर, कासदीपन, श्वासहर, तृष्णानिग्रह, कृमिघ्न, विषहर

rogaghnata: अतिसार (Atisara), क्लैब्य (Klaibya), अग्निमांद्य (Agnimandya), अरुचि (Aruchi), कृमि (Krumi), कास (Kasa), श्वास (Shwasa), पिपासा (Pipasa), शोष (Shosha), प्रमेह (Prameha), केशरोग (Kesharoga). जातीपत्री: अरुचि, वैवर्ण्य, श्वास, तृष्णा, कृमि, विषम रोग, कण्डू, शोष
amayika_prayoga: अतिसार (Atisara) = Paste of Jatiphala applied over umbilicus
व्यङ्ग (Vyanga) = Aril of Jatiphala triturated with sheeta water, applied over face
विपादिका (Vipadika / Cracked feet) = Paste of Jatiphala filled in cracks
अजीर्ण, तृष्णा, सर्दी = Jatiphala Hima
शिरःशूल (Headache) = Local application of Jatiphala paste
सर्दी (Cold) = Jatiphala + Tandulodaka
विपादिका (Crack-heels) = Filled paste of Jatiphala
अनिद्रा (Insomnia) = Jatiphala + Ghrita applied in eyes
plantPhotoAttribution: Wikimedia Commons - Myristica fragrans (Nutmeg on Tree)

name: Brahmi
sloka: ब्राह्मी हिमा सरा तिक्ता लघुर्मेध्या च शीतला | कषाया मधुरा स्वर्या स्मृत्युक्ता कुष्ठनाशिनी | स्वर्या वयस्या कुष्ठोत्थ पाण्डुमेहास्त्रकासजित् | विषशोथ ज्वरहरी || भा. प्र.

external_morphology.habitat: Wetlands, muddy shores, marshy areas throughout India and tropics
details: Stem: Fleshy, rooting at nodes, glabrous
Leaves: Oblanceolate, arranged oppositely, sessile, fleshy
Flowers: Blue, purple, or white, axillary, solitary

phytoconstituents: Alkaloid brahmine (resembles strychnine but less toxic), bacoside A & B, bacopaside, sterols, B-oxalate, B-chloroplatinate
prabhava: Apasmara nashak, Unmad nashak, Vishaghni. Best drug for loss of memory (Agrya karma).

karma.dosha_karma: त्रिदोषनाशक (Tridosha nashaka). Dhatu = Meda, Sarvadhatu Balya. Mala = Mutra, Sreshaya.
karma.karma: दीपन, पाचन, रसायन, स्वर्य, आयुवर्धक, मेध्य, कुष्ठघ्न, कण्डूघ्न, विषघ्न, मेहघ्न, शोथहर, ज्वरघ्न

rogaghnata: उन्माद (Unmada), अपस्मार (Apasmara), कुष्ठ (Kushtha), पाण्डु (Pandu), शोथ (Shotha), प्रमेह (Prameha), श्वास (Shwasa), कास (Kasa), ज्वर (Jwara), स्वरभेद (Swarabheda), अनार्तव (Anartava)
amayika_prayoga: अपस्मार (Apasmara/Epilepsy) = Purana ghrita processed with Brahmi rasa, Vacha + Kushtha + Shankhapushpi
उन्माद (Unmada/Insanity) = Juice of Brahmi, Kushmandi phala, Shadgandha, Shankhapushpi mixed with Kushtha churna + Honey
varieties: Brahmi (Bacopa monnieri), Kshudra Patra Brahmi (Centella asiatica - Mandukaparni)

name: Kalamegha
sloka: श्रुतिकतः लघुष्णशोषः कफावरविनाशनः | दीपनः स्वेदतो ज्ञेयः कृमिघ्नः पित्तसारकः || ध्र. नि

vernacular_names.name: Kalmegh, Green chiretta, King of bitters

external_morphology.habitat: India, especially West Bengal, also throughout tropical Asia
details: Habit: Annual erect herb, 30-110 cm height
Stem: Upper part quadrangular, lower part nearly rounded
Leaves: Glabrous, pinnate, acute apex, entire margin
Flowers: White with rose-purple spots on petals
Fruit: Capsules linear-oblong, acute at both ends

phytoconstituents: Lactones - andrographolide, andrograpnin, panicolin, andrograpolide, neoandrographolide

karma.dosha_karma: कफपित्तहर (Kapha-Pitta hara). Ushna virya → Deepan (Agnimandya). Yakruttejgan → Yakrut vruddhi. Rechan → Vibandha. Krumighna → Krimi.
karma.karma: दीपन, पाचन, कृमिघ्न, रेचन, यकृतोत्तेजक, रक्तशोधक, शोथहर, ज्वरहर, कटुपौष्टिक

rogaghnata: अग्निमांद्य (Agnimandya), यकृतवृद्धि (Yakrut vruddhi), विबन्ध (Vibandha), कृमि (Krimi), जीर्णज्वर (Jirna Jwara), दौर्बल्य (Weakness), रक्तदोष (Raktadosha), शोथ (Shotha)
amayika_prayoga: ज्वर (Jwara) = Kalmegh swarasa + Maricha churna
जीर्णज्वर (Chronic fever) = Kalmegh swarasa + Draksha Asava for chronic mucus discharge with stool & excessive tiredness
छर्दी (Chardi/Vomiting) = Paste of Bhunimba mixed with equal quantity of sugar/honey
varieties: Adulterants: Used as substitute & adulterant for Chirayata (Swertia chirayita)
research_updates: Antibacterial activity: significant against E. coli, S. Typhi, Staphylococcus & Pseudomonas
Anti-microbial against S. aureus, S. Pyogenes

name: Aragwadha
sloka: आरग्वधो गुरुः स्वादुः शीतलः स्रंसनो मृदुः | ज्वरहृद्रोगपित्तास्रवातोदावर्तशूलनुत् || ज्वरे तु सततं पथ्यं कोष्ठशुद्धिकरं परम् ||

details: Fruit: A legume which is very long
Seeds: Flat, albuminous, embedded in pulp

phytoconstituents: Pod pulp: sucrose, amino acids, lignoceric acid. Root bark: sennoside, β-sitosterol. Stem bark and leaves: rhein glycoside

karma.dosha_karma: वातपित्तशामक (Vatapittashamak). Agryakarma: चतुरंगुलो मृदु विरेचनानाम् (induces mild purgation)
karma.karma: Fruits: Sramsana (Mridu Virechana), Shulahara, Ruchya, Kushthaghna, Krimighna, Pramehaghna, Jwaraghna | Leaf: Medovishoshaka, Virechana | Flower: Grahi

rogaghnata: Phala (fruit): Jwara, Hridroga, Raktapitta, Udavarta, Kushtha, Krimi, Prameha, Mutrakruchra, Gulma, Udara, Vrana | Patra (leaf): Medoroga
amayika_prayoga: प्रमेह (Prameha/Diabetes) = Aragwadha Kashaya is best for Haridra Meha
कुष्ठ (skin disease) = Leaves of Aragwadha and Kasamarda triturated with buttermilk, paste applied to skin
आमवात (Rheumatoid Arthritis) = Leaves of Aragwadha fried in mustard oil, chewed after evening meal
विसर्प (Erysipelas) = Paste of Aragwadha leaves and Shleshmataka bark mixed with Ghee

name: Lajjalu
sloka: लज्जाळुः शीतला तिक्ता कषाया कफपित्तजित् | रक्तपित्तमतिसारं योनिरोगान्विनाशयेत् ||
classification_of_gana: सुश्रुत, वाग्भट: प्रियङ्ग्वादि गण, अम्बष्ठादि गण

details: Stem and branches: Sparingly prickly, clothed with long weak bristles from bulbous base

phytoconstituents: Seeds contain caesalpin, saponin, fatty oil, mimosine. Leaves: β-sitosterol, tannin, γ-sitosterol

karma.karma: Atisarahara, Raktapittahara, Yoniroganashaka, Shothahara, Dahashamana, Shwasahara, Vranahara, Kushthaghna, Sandhaniya, Raktashodhaka, Pramehaghna, Jwarahara

rogaghnata: Yoniroga, Atisara, Raktapitta, Shopha, Daha, Kushtha, Vrana, Bhagna, Bhagandara
amayika_prayoga: Urustambha: Combination of Samanga, Shalmali and Bilwa taken with Honey
Antravruddhi (hernia): Paste of Lajjalu root and eagle excreta applied externally

name: Dadima
sloka: तत्तु स्वादु त्रिदोषघ्नं तृड्दाहज्वरनाशनम् | कण्ठौष्ठमुखरोगघ्नं तर्पणं शुक्रलं लघु || कषायमधुरं ग्राहि स्निग्धं मेधाबलप्रदम् | अम्लं तु पित्तलमनिलं कफवातहरं लघु ||
classification_of_gana: चरक: हृद्य वर्ग, छर्दिनिग्रहण वर्ग, श्रमहर वर्ग

details: Habit: A large deciduous shrub / small tree
Flower: Solitary, red colour, inferior ovary
Seeds: Watery outer coat containing pink juice, horny inner coat

useful_parts: Phala Twak (fruit rind), Skandha Twak (stem bark), Mula Twak (root bark), Flowers
phytoconstituents: Fruit: glucose, fructose, maltose, carotene, vitamin C, pectin, tannin, punicic acid

rasa_panchaka.rasa: Madhura Dadima: Madhura, Kashaya, Amla | Amla Dadima: Amla
rasa_panchaka.guna: Madhura Dadima: Laghu, Snigdha | Amla Dadima: Laghu, Ruksha
rasa_panchaka.virya: Madhura Dadima: Anushna | Amla Dadima: Ushna
rasa_panchaka.vipaka: Madhura Dadima: Madhura | Amla Dadima: Amla

karma.karma: General: Kasahara, Vranahara, Tarpana, Shukrala, Medhya, Balya, Hrudya | Madhura-Amla Dadima Phala: Deepan, Ruchya | Amla Dadima Phala: Deepan, Ruchya | Dadima Phala Twak: Grahi

rogaghnata: Madhura Dadima Phala: Trishna, Daha, Jwara, Mukha Daurgandhya | Madhura-Amla Dadima Phala: Agnimandya, Aruchi | Amla Dadima Phala: Hrudroga | Dadima Phala Twak: Atisara | Dadima Mula Twak: Krimi
amayika_prayoga: Atisara: Ripe Dadima fruit cooked in closed heating, juice extracted from fruit taken
Raktapitta: Juice of Dadima mixed with juice of Amra leaves, used both externally and internally
Raktarshas (bleeding piles): Decoction of Dadima Phala Twak
व्यंग (Pigmentation): Fresh rind of Dadima triturated with Aja Kshira (goat milk), applied externally
तृष्णा (Thirst): Paste of Sugar + Dadima kept in mouth reduces dryness
varieties: भावप्रकाश: 1) Swadu 2) Swaduamla 3) Amla. धन्वन्तरि निघण्टु: 1) Madhura Dadima 2) Amla Dadima. Flowers used in PCOD. Avaleha yoga: Loha + Abhraka + Dadima avaleha used for iron deficiency and pernicious anemia

name: Eranda
sloka: एरण्डमूलादुष्णं गुरु विनाशयेत् | शूलशोथकटीबस्तीशिरः पीडोदकज्वरान् || द्विविधस्यामवातस्य कण्डूकुष्ठविनाशनम् | तन्मज्जा च विडभेदी वातश्लेष्मदोषहरः ||
classification_of_gana: चरक: भेदनीय वर्ग, स्वेदोपग वर्ग, अंगमर्दप्रशमन वर्ग

details: Habit: A tall glabrous annual shrub
Leaf: Alternate, broad palmately lobed and veined, lobes (7-8), simple, serrate, reticulate venation

phytoconstituents: Seed: fixed oil (45-52%), proteids 20%, starch, albumin, ricin (toxic), uric acid. Root and root bark: potassium, sodium, magnesium, chloride. Leaves: lysine, leucine, valine, ricinine, rutin

karma.dosha_karma: त्रिदोषशामक (Tridoshashamak). Agryakarma: Root of Eranda is best Aphrodisiac and pacifies Vata.
karma.karma: Eranda Mula: Vrishya, Shulahara, Shothahara, Shirapidahara, Jwarahara, Anahahara, Kasanashaka, Kushthaghna | Eranda Beeja: Bhedana, Shulahara, Deepan, Ushna, Udarshoolahara, Krimighna, Mutrakruchrahara | Eranda Patra: Krimighna, Kushthahara, Bastishoolahara

rogaghnata: Eranda Mula: Shula, Katishula, Bastishula, Amavata, Vatavyadhi, Pakshaghata, Shotha, Shirahshula, Jwara, Gulma, Anaha, Kushtha | Eranda Beeja: Vatavyadhi, Agnimandya, Udara, Mutrakruchra | Eranda Patra: Krimi, Mutrakruchra, Bastishula | Eranda Pushpa: Mutradosha
amayika_prayoga: आमवात (Rheumatoid Arthritis) = Eranda Taila
पार्श्वशूल (pain in flanks) = Kwatha of 80g Eranda mula with 8x water, administered with Yavakshara and double quantity Triphala kwatha with milk
विबन्ध (Constipation) = Eranda Taila mixed with double quantity Triphala kwatha with milk
उदर रोग (Ascites) = Eranda Taila
शूल (Abdominal pain) = Kwatha of Shunthi and Erandmula mixed with Hingu and Saindhava lavana
अर्शस् (Piles) = Kwatha of Ashta (?) and Eranda
varieties: भावप्रकाश: 1) श्वेत (White) 2) रक्त (Red). राजनिघण्टु: 1) श्वेत 2) रक्त 3) स्थूल. Note on toxicity: seed fatal dose is 5-10 crushed seeds, fatal period 2 to several days.
research_updates: Ricin used in cancer chemotherapy as immunotoxins

name: Shigru
sloka: शिग्रुः कटुः कटुः पाके तीक्ष्णोष्णो मधूरो लघुः | दीपनो रोचनो जन्तुः क्षारस्तिक्तो विदाहकृत् || संग्राही शुकोलो हृद्यः पित्तरक्तप्रकोपणः | चक्षुष्यः कफवातनः हन्ति शोथप्लीहग्रन्थ्यव्रणार्बुदम् ||
classification_of_gana: चरक: स्वेदोपग वर्ग, शिरोविरेचनोपग वर्ग, कटुकस्कन्ध

external_morphology.habitat: Cultivated in India
details: Habit: Small to middle sized tree
Bark: Corky; wood soft; root tomentose

phytoconstituents: Whole plant: amino acids like leucine, aspartic acid, glutamic acid, glycine, valine. Organic acids: citric, malic, malonic, oxalic. Leaves: high iron, potassium, vitamin C. Pods: protein, calcium, copper, iodine, oxalic acid

karma.karma: Deepan, Bhedani, Shukrala, Hrudya, Chakshushya, Krimighna, Vishaghna, Shwayathuhara, Vranahara, Medohara, Kandughna, Shoolahara, Shwasahara, Mukharujahara, Vatashoolanashana

rogaghnata: Shigru: Netraroga, Vishavikara, Shotha, Krimi, Kushthahara, Medoroga, Shwasa, Pakshaghata, Ardhangavata, Kasa, Udararoga | Shweta Shigru: Shopha, Aruchi, Agnimandya | Rakta Shigru: Shopha; used as a Rasayana
amayika_prayoga: नेत्ररोग (eye disease) = Juice of leaves alleviates diseases of the eye caused by vitiation of Vata, Pitta, Kapha, or all three doshas
शिरःशूल (Headache) = Nasya with juice of Shigru leaves mixed with Jaggery
कृमी रोग (worm infestation) = Decoction of Shigru and Vidanga mixed with Honey
varieties: भावप्रकाश: 1) Shigru 2) Shweta 3) Rakta 4) Nila. Madhu Shigru: white flower (Moringa concanensis). Kata Shigru: red flower (Moringa oleifera). Other uses: leaf juice externally for muscle spasm; root bark Lepa for Vranashodhana; seed powder with Vidanga for Krimi

name: Bala
sloka: बला स्निग्धा हिमा स्वादुर्वृष्या बल्या त्रिदोषनुत् | रक्तपित्तं क्षयं हन्ति बलौजो वर्धयत्यपि ||
classification_of_gana: चरक: बृंहणीय वर्ग, बल्य, प्रजास्थापन वर्ग
phytoconstituents: Seed: ephedrine, fatty oil, steroids, resin, resin acids. Root: vasicine, vasicinol, betaine

karma.dosha_karma: वातपित्तशामक (Vatapittashamak). Agryakarma: Bala is astringent, an energy promoter, and pacifies Vata.
karma.karma: Balya, Kantikara, Grahi, Vrishya, Ojovardhaka, Ayurvardhaka, Stambhana, Brumhana, Shothahara, Rasayana, Hrudya

rogaghnata: Vatavyadhi, Daurbalya, Kshaya, Raktapitta, Vatarakta, Arsha, Mutratisara, Mutrakruchra | Balaphala: Adhmana, Jwara
amayika_prayoga: वातव्याधी (Vatavyadhi) = Bala Taila
वातरक्त (Gout) = Bala Taila used for bath, enema and diet in Vatarakta
मदात्ययजन्य तृष्णा (thirst caused by alcoholism) = Water processed with Bala given to drink
श्लीपद (Filariasis) = Milk processed with two types of Bala taken in the morning
varieties: 4 types of Bala (per भावप्रकाश and Kaiyadeva Nighantu): Bala, Atibala, Nagabala, Mahabala

name: Ashwagandha
sloka: अश्वगन्धाऽनिलश्लेष्मश्वित्रशोथक्षयापहा | बल्या रसायनी तिक्ता कषायोष्णातिशुक्रला ||

details: Fruit: Small berry, red when ripe

phytoconstituents: Somniferine, somniferinine, withanine, nicotine, withaniol

karma.karma: Balya, Kantivardhaka, Shwasahara, Shothahara, Rasayana, Kasahara, Kandughna, Vibandhahara, Shukrala, Nadibalya, Keshya, Vranahara

rogaghnata: वातव्याधि, शोष, विषम ज्वर, व्रण, कास, शोथ, कण्डू, केश्य समस्याएँ, श्वास
amayika_prayoga: रसायन (Rejuvenation) = Ashwagandha taken with milk/ghee/oil or warm water for 15 days acts as Rasayana
कृशता (Emaciation) = Ghrita prepared from Ashwagandha taken every morning
श्वास (Dyspnoea) = Kshara of Ashwagandha taken with Honey and Ghee
निद्रानाश (Insomnia) = Ashwagandha churna mixed with sugar and ghee
गर्भधारणार्थ (For conception) = Ghrita prepared from Ashwagandha kwatha and milk consumed at the right time to aid conception

name: Bhumyamalaki
sloka: भूम्यामलकी हिमा तिक्ता कषाया मधुरा हिमा | नेत्री पाण्डुप्रणीला हिक्का श्वासकफास्त्रहा ||

details: Habit: An annual herb, grows 30-60 cm in height

phytoconstituents: Bitter substances and alkaloids; whole plant contains phyllanthin, phyllinim, phyllantheol

karma.karma: Kasahara, Kandughna, Dahahara, Rochaka, Kushthaghna, Shwasahara, Trishnahara, Shothahara, Balahara, Mutraroga-hara

rogaghnata: श्वास, कास, कामला, पाण्डु, शोथ, प्रमेह, मूत्रविकार, कुष्ठ, कण्डू, तृष्णा, दाह
amayika_prayoga: प्रदर (Menorrhagia) = 20g Bhumyamalaki + 20 Maricha, powder of Bhumyamalaki root taken with rice water cures Pradara quickly
plantPhotoAttribution: Wikimedia Commons - Phyllanthus urinaria / Phyllanthus amarus

name: Ashoka
sloka: अशोकः शीतलस्तिक्तो ग्राही वर्ण्यः कषायकः | दोषापचीतुषाहार्त्तिशोथशूलविषापहृत् ||

details: Habit: A tree, grows 6-9 m high, branches are glabrous

phytoconstituents: Bark: tannin (6%), catechol. Pods: catechol. Seeds: palmitic and stearic acid. Flowers: γ-sitosterol, gallic acid

karma.karma: Grahi, Varnya, Trishnahara, Dahashamak, Krimighna, Vranaropaka, Raktya, Sandhaniya, Vishaghna, Shleshmanashana, Jwaraghna

rogaghnata: प्रदर, योनिव्यापत्, तृष्णा, दाह, कृमी, व्रण, विषरोग, शोथ, अपची, अर्श, गुल्म, उदरशूल
amayika_prayoga: वातव्याधि (Vata disorders) = Ashoka Ghrita
प्रदर (Menorrhagia) = Ashoka Twak Kwatha with cold milk in the morning checks severe bleeding per vaginam
अश्मरी (Urinary calculi) = Ashoka Bija Churna with water relieves Mutraghata and Ashmari
Ashoka Kashaya = Swasada
plantPhotoAttribution: Wikimedia Commons - Saraca indica / Saraca asoca

name: Nagakesar
sloka: नागपुष्पं कषायोष्णं रूक्षं लघु च पाचकम् | ज्वरकण्डूतृषास्वेदछर्दिहृल्लासनाशनम् || रोगिणोऽप्यनुकूलवीर्यं कफवातविनाशनम् ||

synonyms.meaning: Petals resemble the hood of a cobra
Stamens have a yellow colour like Champaka flower
Stamens are very useful as medicine

classification_of_gana: सुश्रुत: एलादि गण, प्रियङ्ग्वादि, अंजनादि गण

external_morphology.habitat: Grows commonly in Western Ghats, Eastern Ghats, Bengal and Assam
details: Fruit: Ovoid shaped with a conical point

phytoconstituents: Flower bud: mesuol, mammeisin, mammeigin. Stamens: β-sitosterol, β-amyrine

karma.karma: Pachana, Jwaraghna, Kandughna, Trishnahara, Keshya, Dourgandhyahara, Shamaka, Chhedana, Swedopaha, Vidahaka

rogaghnata: अरुची, ज्वर, कण्डू, त्वक् रोग, अतिस्वेद, छर्दी, हृल्लास, दौर्गन्ध्य, कुष्ठ, विसर्प, विषरोग, बस्तिरुजा, वातरक्त, तृष्णा, अधोग रक्तपित्त (रक्तदोष)
amayika_prayoga: रक्तार्शस् (bleeding piles) = Nagakesar mixed with butter and sugar
हिक्का (Hiccough) = Nagakesar mixed with sugar, honey, sugarcane juice and Madhuka
रक्तातिसार (blood-mixed diarrhoea) = Use of Nagakesar powder
varieties: Substitutes: 1) Rakta Nagakeshara 2) Krishna Nagakeshara 3) Malabar Nagakeshara

name: Karpura
sloka: कर्पूरः शीतलो वृष्यः चक्षुष्यो लेखनो लघुः | सुरभितिर्वरस्तिक्तः कफपित्तविनाशनः ||

external_morphology.habitat: Native of China, Japan and Taiwan

phytoconstituents: Camphor, campherol, cineol, camphene, terpineol, safrole, reticuline

karma.karma: Kantadoshahara, Vishahara, Chakshushya, Dahahara, Vrishya, Medhya, Krimihanashana, Sugandhodbhava, Mukhavairasyahara

rogaghnata: Not mentioned in notes
amayika_prayoga: Chest congestion = Camphor dissolved in oil (5 g camphor in 100 ml warm sesame oil), applied over chest
Cough/cold = Camphor mixed with honey for cough and throat irritation (500 mg-1 g camphor powder with a spoon of honey)
Flatulence/Indigestion/Headache/Anorexia = Equal parts camphor, rock salt, cumin seeds, cardamom and clove made into a fine paste and rolled into pills
kalpana: Gandhaka Malahara - ointment for itching skin
research_updates: Camphor poisoning: overdose may lead to seizures, confusion, restlessness, nausea

name: Ativisha
sloka: विषा सोष्णकषायोक्ता दीपिनी पाचनी हरेत् | कफपित्तातिसारघ्नी वयःस्था शूलहरी कटुः ||
classification_of_gana: सुश्रुत: पिप्पल्यादि गण, वचादि, मुस्तादि गण
phytoconstituents: Root: atisine, atidine, atodine, aconitic acid

karma.dosha_karma: कफपित्तशामक (Kaphapittashamak). Agryakarma: Appetizer, Digestant, Astringent, pacifies all three Doshas
karma.karma: Deepan, Pachan, Kasahara, Jwarahara, Krimighna, Vishahara, Chardihara

rogaghnata: Atisara, Agnimandya, Ajirna, Jwara, Kasa, Chardi, Krimi, Baladaurbalya, Visharoga
amayika_prayoga: कास (Cough) = Ativisha Kanda Churna administered with honey
आमातिसार (diarrhoea with undigested food) = Peya processed with Ativisha, Nagara and sour substances
ग्रहणी (Sprue) = Kwatha of Ativisha, Shunthi and Musta, useful in digesting Ama
varieties: मदनपाल निघण्टु: 1) Rakta 2) Shweta 3) Krishna 4) Pita

name: Jiraka (Shweta)
sloka: जीरकद्वयं कटु पाके पाचनं दीपनं लघु | गर्भाशयविशुद्धिकरं बल्यं च सुगन्धिकम् || तिक्तं विशेषाद् नयनं वात कुष्ठ विसर्प ज्वरघ्नं ||
phytoconstituents: Fruit: 2.5-4% volatile oil and proteins; cuminic alcohol, cuminaldehyde, thymol

karma.karma: दीपन, संग्राही, मेध्य, गर्भाशय शोधक, ज्वरघ्न, पाचन, वृष्य, बल्य, वेद्य, चक्षुष्य, हृद्य, हिक्कानिग्रह, छेदन, लेखन, व्रणशोधन

rogaghnata: अग्निमांद्य, अजीर्ण, अतिसार, विषमज्वर, छर्दी, अम्लपित्त, कण्डू, कृमी, नेत्ररोग, आध्मान, गुल्म, हृद्रोग, गर्भाशय विकार, उदरशूल
amayika_prayoga: ज्वर (Fever) = Paste of Jiraka with Jaggery
अम्लपित्त (Hyperacidity) = Jeeraka, Dhanyaka - siddha shrota
छर्दी (Vomiting) = Sauvarchala salt, Ajaji, sugar, maricha mixed with honey stops vomiting
research_updates: Jeeraka Phanta used for cleaning wounds
Jeeraka Lepa used for scorpion bite
Jaggery + Jeeraka churna used for Stanya Vriddhi
Anticancer activity relevant to colon cancer
Contains vitamin E, improves skin tone and texture

name: Dhataki
sloka: धातकी कटुका शीता मदकृत्कुष्ठनाशिनी | तृष्णातिसारपित्तास्त्रकृमिकासविषप्रणुत् ||
phytoconstituents: Flower: gallic acid, β-sitosterol, tannins. Leaves: betulin, ursolic acid

karma.karma: तृषाहर, वमनघ्न, विबन्ध, कृमिघ्न, विसर्पहर, गर्भस्थापन, मदकर, व्रणहर, संधानीय

rogaghnata: अतिसार, तृष्णा, कृमी, विसर्प, रक्तातिसार, रक्तार्शस्, रक्तपित्त, विषविकार, प्रवाहिका, व्रण
amayika_prayoga: बालातिसार (diarrhoea in children) = Combination of Dhataki Pushpa, sugar and Laja mixed together
प्रवाहिका (Dysentery) = Juice of Dhataki flower, Badari leaf, Kapittha mixed with Lodhra churna, honey and buttermilk
पित्ताभिष्यन्द (conjunctivitis due to Pitta) = Churna of Dhataki and Chandana mixed with breast milk applied as Anjana

name: Dhanvayasa
sloka: धन्वयासो मधूरस्तिक्तस्तुवरः शीतलो लघुः | तृष्णादाहकफभ्रान्तिपित्तज्वरविनाशकृत् ||

external_morphology.habitat: Mainly grows in dry regions of North-western India, Rajasthan and Andhra Pradesh

phytoconstituents: Chinovic acid, harmine, alanine, glycine, lysine

karma.dosha_karma: वातपित्तशामक (Vatapittashamak). Agryakarma: Pacifies Pitta and Kapha
karma.karma: तृषाहर, कुष्ठघ्न, वीर्यनाशक, ज्वरहर, छर्दीहर, भ्रमजित्, बालग्रहादिनाशक

rogaghnata: कुष्ठ, विसर्प, ज्वर, तृष्णा, दाह, छर्दी, रक्तपित्त, वातरक्त, मद, भ्रम
amayika_prayoga: रक्तपित्त (Raktapitta) = Roots of Yavasa

name: Gokshura
sloka: गोक्षुरः शीतलः स्वादुर्बलकृद्बस्तिशोधनः | मधुरो दीपनो वृष्यः पुष्टिश्चाश्मरीहा ||
classification_of_gana: सुश्रुत: वीरतर्वादि गण, विरेचनादि गण, लघुपंचमूल

details: Root and fruits are the parts used

phytoconstituents: Harogenin, harmine, rutin

rasa_panchaka.guna: Phala: Guru, Snigdha | Mula: Guru, Snigdha

karma.karma: शोथहर, मूत्रल, बस्तिशोधक, बल्य, वृष्य, अश्मरीहर, प्रमेहहर, श्वासहर, कासहर

rogaghnata: मूत्रकृच्छ्र, अश्मरी, शोथ, प्रमेह, श्वास, कास, हृद्रोग, क्लैब्य
amayika_prayoga: मूत्रकृच्छ्र (Dysuria) = Gokshura Kwatha taken with sugar
अश्मरी (Urinary calculi) = Churna mixed with honey and goat's milk given for 7 days to break Ashmari
केशवर्धनार्थ (to promote hair growth) = Flowers of Gokshura and Tila mixed with equal quantity of honey and ghee, applied on scalp
आमवात (RA) + कटिशूल (Lumbago) = Kwatha of Shunthi and Gokshura taken regularly in the morning acts as a digestant and relieves pain
research_updates: Management of sexual dysfunction in males

name: Kulatha
sloka: कुलत्थः कटुकः पाके कषायः पित्तरक्तकृत् | लघुर्विदाही वीर्योष्णः श्वासकासकफानिलान् || श्लेष्माश्मरीमेदोहरो हिक्कामेदोज्वरान् ||

synonyms.meaning: The creeper spreads on the ground
Kwatha has Lekhana Karma, reduces weight

classification_of_gana: चरक: स्वेदोपग वर्ग, वातशमन, धान्य वर्ग

phytoconstituents: Strepogenin, γ-sitosterol, genistein, D-glucose, D-galactose

karma.karma: अश्मरीहर, श्वासहर, कासहर, लेखन, मूत्रल, स्वेदजनन, मेदोज्वरहर, ज्वरघ्न, कृमिघ्न, केशीकर, अंजन, त्वचीकर, शूलहर

rogaghnata: अश्मरी, श्वास, कास, मेदरोग, ज्वर, अरुची, अर्श, शूल, व्रण
amayika_prayoga: अतिस्वेद (excessive sweating) = Kulatha powder rubbed over the body to reduce Atisweda
कास व श्वास = Ghee cooked with decoction of Kulatha, Pippali, Pippali root, Chavya and Shringavera is very useful in Kasa, Shwasa, Hikka

name: Lavanga
sloka: लवङ्गं देवकुसुमं तीक्ष्णं श्रीसृष्टिनुत्तमम् | लवङ्गं कटुकं तिक्तं लघु नेत्रहितं हिमम् || दीपनं पाचनं रुच्यं कफातिरुजानाशनकृत् || तृष्णां छर्दि तथानाहं शूलमाशु विनाशयेत् || कासं श्वासञ्च हिक्काञ्च क्षयञ्चाशु विनाशयेत् ||

external_morphology.habitat: Cultivated mainly in Tamil Nadu and Kerala; abundantly found in Sri Lanka, Mauritius, Tanzania and Singapore
details: Leaves: Simple, bright green and glossy, lower surface has aromatic oil glands
Flowers: Small clusters, bright red colour
Fruit: Long red ovary of flower gradually turns reddish-purple
Seed: Fruit contains 1 seed, rarely 2 seeds

phytoconstituents: Eugenol, eugenine, eugenone, vanillin

karma.karma: मुखशोधन, दुर्गन्धहर, कृमिदन्त (relieves dental ache), वातानुलोमन, शूलप्रशमन, ग्रहणीरोगहर, सृष्टिज (auspicious)

rogaghnata: अग्निमांद्य, शूल, छर्दी, तृष्णा, कास, श्वास, हिक्का, क्षयग्रह, ज्वर, नेत्ररोग, कुष्ठ, संन्यास
amayika_prayoga: वमन (Vomiting) = Lavanga pounded and applied with warm water externally
शिरशूल, प्रतिश्याय = Local application of paste
दन्तशूल (Toothache) = Clove oil paste
विसूचिका (Cholera) = Water boiled with clove, filtered and given
तृष्णा (Thirst) = Fresh juice
आनाह, आध्मान = Powder of 1 g roasted clove with honey
छर्दी (Vomiting) = Clove powder with honey
आमज छर्दी = Lavanga Phanta
तालुशोष श्वास = Lavanga Phanta with honey
ज्वर (Fever) = Churna
varieties: Adulterants: Mother cloves, blown cloves, clove stalks, exhausted cloves
research_updates: Antiviral activity against Herpes simplex virus

name: Nirgundi
sloka: सिन्दुवारः सुगन्धोऽथ तिक्तोष्णः कफवातहा | केश्यो नेत्रहितो हन्ति शूलशोथाममारुतान् || सिन्दुवारदलं जन्तुवातप्रशमनं लघु ||

synonyms.meaning: Aroma of Nirgundi spreads all around
Juice extracted from leaves is an efficacious medicine
Has Ushna Virya, hence can alleviate Sheetatva

external_morphology.habitat: All over India

phytoconstituents: Bark: vanillic acid, luteolin, β-sitosterol. Seed oil: vanillin, d-α-pinene, alanine, valine, camphene

karma.karma: Cures many diseases and protects the body

rogaghnata: शूल, शोथ, कृमी, वातव्याधि, कुष्ठ, कण्डू, केश्य विकार, प्रदर, संघातवात, गलगण्ड, आनाह
amayika_prayoga: वातव्याधि = 1 Karsha (12g) powder of Nirgundi taken with oil, useful in Sandhivata, Katiwata, Kampavata (Amavata, trembling)
कास (Cough) = Ghee processed with juice of Nirgundi leaves is efficacious in Kasa
अपस्मार (Epilepsy) = Powder of Nirgundi taken internally

name: Nimba
sloka: निम्बः शीतो लघुग्राही कटुकोऽग्नीवातकृत | हृल्लासकुष्ठपित्तकफज्वरकृमीप्रणूत ||
classification_of_gana: सुश्रुत: आरग्वधादि गण, गुडुच्यादि, लाक्षादि गण

karma.karma: Leaf: कृमिघ्न, नेत्र्य, विषघ्न, कुष्ठघ्न, कण्डूघ्न, कासहर, श्वासहर | Bark: कुष्ठघ्न, कृमिघ्न, श्रमहर, तृष्णाहर, ज्वरघ्न, प्रदरहर, कण्ठ्य, स्तन्यशोधक | Fruit: कुष्ठघ्न, कृमिघ्न, प्रमेह, गुल्म, भेदक | Seed: कृमिघ्न, कुष्ठहर | Flower: कृमिघ्न, विषघ्न

amayika_prayoga: कुष्ठ (Psoriasis) = Nimba + Vidanga powder mixed with cow's urine, applied; also used internally
सुरामेह (Acetonuria) = Decoction of Nimba used internally
त्वग्रोग (skin disease) = Nimba + Aragwadha applied on skin
अरुंषिका (head boils) = Blood letting first, then decoction of Nimba sprinkled over head boils

name: Madanaphala
sloka: मदनो मधुरस्तिक्तस्तिक्ष्णोष्णो लेखनो लघुः | वमनो विद्रधीहरः कफकुष्ठानिलापहः ||

synonyms.meaning: Madana is the best Vamaka Dravya

classification_of_gana: चरक: आस्थापनोपग वर्ग, अनुवासनोपग वर्ग, वमनोपग वर्ग

external_morphology.habitat: Grows throughout India, very common in hilly regions

amayika_prayoga: वमनार्थ (to induce emesis) = Madana is the best drug for inducing emesis

पार्श्वशूल (pain in flanks) = Madana Kalka prepared from sour gruel applied over navel relieves various pains including flank pain

name: Mandookaparni

synonyms.meaning: Runner/growth pattern resembles how a frog jumps
Imparts good knowledge by enhancing memory

external_morphology.habitat: Grows in moist regions
details: Leaf: Simple, arising in groups from nodes

phytoconstituents: Centelloside, centic acid, centellic acid, centoic acid

karma.karma: मेध्य, रसायन, स्वर्य, कान्तिवर्धक, कुष्ठघ्न, कासहर, विषहर, शोथहर, ज्वरहर, हृद्य, आयुष्यकर, कण्डूघ्न, बस्तिरोगहर, श्वासहर

amayika_prayoga: मेध्य (intellect promoter) = Regular use of Mandookaparni fried with ghee for a month promotes intellect
कास (cough) and शोष (wasting) = Combination of Mandookaparni, Yashti, Vishwabheshaja taken starting at 5g, gradually increased to 10g over a month
स्वरविकार (voice disorders) = For clear voice, Mandookaparni, Madhuka mula, Ameya, Shankhapushpi mixed with honey

external_morphology.habitat: Hilly areas

rogaghnata: शूल, त्वचारोग, विसर्प, वैवर्ण्य, यकृद्विकार, प्रमेह, योनीरोग, शोथ, अरुची, नेत्ररोग, अपची, अतिसार, व्रण, ज्वर

प्रमेह (Prameha) = Decoction of Manjishtha and Chandana
भग्न (Fracture) = Paste of Manjishtha and Madhuka triturated with sour gruel applied to heal fracture

name: Maricha
sloka: मरीचं कटुकं तीक्ष्णं दीपनं कफवातनुत् | उष्णं पित्तकरं रूक्षं श्वासशूलकृमीन्हरेत् ||

external_morphology.habitat: Indigenous to the western coast of India, grown all over South India
details: Fruits: Globose, green turning to red colour when fully ripe

phytoconstituents: Alkaloid (5-9%), volatile oil (1-2.5%), starch (volatile oil contains β-phellandrene, camphene, caryophyllene)

rasa_panchaka.guna: Shushka: Tikshna, Laghu | Ardra: Guru, Tikshna

karma.karma: दीपन, श्वासहर, हिक्कानिग्रह, शूलहर, रोचन, हृद्रोगहर, लेखन, चक्षुष्य, प्रमेहहर, कृमिहर, विषहर, शिरोविरेचन

rogaghnata: अग्निमांद्य, शूल, कृमी, कास, श्वास, हृद्रोग, वातव्याधि, मूत्रकृच्छ्र, नेत्ररोग, विषरोग | Sheeta Maricha: विषघ्न, चक्षुष्य, रसायन, नेत्रशूलहर
amayika_prayoga: प्रवाहिका (Dysentery) = Fine powder of Pippali + Maricha
ग्रहणी (Sprue) = Powder of Maricha, Chitrak, Sauvarchala Lavana taken with buttermilk
कास (Cough) = Fine powder of Maricha mixed with ghee, honey and sugar cures all kinds of cough

name: Meshashringi
sloka: मेषशृङ्गीफलं तिक्तं कुष्ठमेहकफप्रणुत् | दीपनं श्वासकासास्रविषहा लघू पाचनी || तद्वीजं परमं वृष्यं वातशूलहरं लघु ||

synonyms.meaning: Fruits resemble horns of a goat
Fruits resemble horns of a goat
Eating the leaves temporarily removes the ability to taste sweetness

details: Habit: Large woody, much-branched climber running over the tops of high trees
Flowers: Green, in sessile cymes, yellow

phytoconstituents: Gymnamine, alanine, valine, adenine, betaine

karma.karma: मेहघ्न, श्वासहर, कासहर, व्रणहर, अश्रिशूलहर | Others: कृमिघ्न, अक्षिरोगहर, विषविकारहर

rogaghnata: मेह, श्वास, कास, व्रण, अश्रिशूल, कृमी, अक्षिरोग, विषविकार
amayika_prayoga: मधुमेह (Diabetes) = Meshashringi churna
सन्धिशोथ (joint swelling) = Leaf paste of Meshashringi applied over joints

name: Methika
sloka: मेथिका कटुरुष्णा च रक्तपित्तप्रकोपणी | अरुचिज्वरशमन दीपिनी वातशूलघ्नी ||

details: Fruits: Pods, 5-7.5 cm long, with a long persistent beak, often falcate, 10-20 seeded

karma.karma: ज्वरघ्न, कान्तिकर, दीपन, नाडीबल्य, बल्य, शूलहर, वृष्य

rogaghnata: वातव्याधि, ज्वर, अग्निमांद्य, नाडीदौर्बल्य, दौर्बल्य, शूल, गृध्रसी, श्वेतप्रदर
amayika_prayoga: वातव्याधि = Powder of Chaturbija (Methika, Chandrashura, Kalajaji, Yavani) taken to destroy all Vatavyadhi
अजीर्ण, शूल, आध्मान, पार्श्वशूल, कटिवात

name: Musta
sloka: मुस्तं हिमं कटु ग्राहि तिक्तं दीपनपाचनम् | कषायं कफपित्तास्त्रतृड्ज्वरारुचिजन्तुजित् ||

synonyms.meaning: Commonly grows near the banks of the river Ganga
Common in the north-eastern region of India

classification_of_gana: चरक: तृप्तिघ्न वर्ग, तृष्णानिग्रहण, लेखनीय, कण्डूघ्न

external_morphology.habitat: Grows like a weed in waste fields and water resources
details: Leaves: Basal, lower ones often scale-like

phytoconstituents: Tubers yield cyperene, α-selinine, cyperone, keto-alcohols

karma.dosha_karma: पित्तकफशामक (Pittakaphashamak). Agryakarma: Astringent, Appetizer and Digestant
karma.karma: शीतग्राही, ज्वरघ्न, त्वचाहर, रोचक, कृमिघ्न, स्तन्यशोधन, पाचक, दीपन

rogaghnata: अतिसार, ज्वर, अरुची, दाह, तृष्णा, कृमी
amayika_prayoga: अतिसार (Diarrhoea) = Decoction of Musta root taken with honey checks all kinds of diarrhoea
ज्वर (Fever) = Decoction and cold infusion of Musta and Parpata
हालीमक (chronic obstructive jaundice) = Iron powder + Musta churna taken with Kashaya of Khadira
वातरक्त (Vatarakta) = Continuous use of decoction of Musta, Amalaki, Nisha taken with honey
kalpana: Mustadi Churna - श्वास, पाण्डु, कामला, ग्रहणी

name: Palasha
sloka: पलाशो दीपनो वृष्यः सरोष्णो व्रणगुल्मजित् | कषायः कटुकस्तिक्तः स्निग्धो गुदरुजापहृत् || भग्नसंधानकृद्ग्राही कट्वोष्णः कफवातजित् ||
classification_of_gana: सुश्रुत: लोध्रादि गण, मुष्ककादि गण, न्यग्रोधादि

details: Habit: Erect tree, grows up to 50 ft, trunk crooked

useful_parts: Seed, Leaf, Flower, Bark, Gum, Root bark, Flower (Keshar)
phytoconstituents: Seed: palasonin, aleuritic acid. Flowers: butin, isobutin, isocoreopsin. Root bark: β-sitosterol, tannic acid

rasa_panchaka.rasa: Seed: Tikta, Kashaya, Katu | Flower: Madhura

karma.karma: Seed: Anthelmintic, laxative | Leaves and flower: Tonic, aphrodisiac, diuretic | Other: कफपित्तशामक (for flower), कुष्ठघ्न, जन्तुघ्न, व्रणहर, अर्शहर

doshaghnata: Seed and Bark: वातकफशामक | Flower: कफपित्तशामक
rogaghnata: कृमी, श्वास, कास, कुष्ठ, विसर्प, दाह, गुल्म, अर्श, नेत्ररोग
amayika_prayoga: कृमीरोग (worm infestation) = Paste and juice of Palasha seed taken with rice water
ज्वर में दाह (fever with burning) = Paste of tender leaves of Palasha, Nimba and Badara
पैत्तिक शूल (Colic) = Soup prepared from Palasha, Dhanyaka and sugar
varieties: 1) Rakta Palasha 2) Pita Palasha 3) Shweta Palasha 4) Nila Palasha

name: Pashanbheda
sloka: अश्मभेदो हिमस्तिक्तः कषायो बस्तिशोधनः | भेदनो हन्ति दोषार्शोगुल्मकृच्छ्रअश्मरीरुजः ||

synonyms.meaning: Breaks open the ground/sprout, best among drugs useful in Ashmari

external_morphology.habitat: Grows in Himalayan region at about 6000 ft altitude
details: Habit: Branched perennial herb, grows 60-180 cm in height
Root: Red in colour, 2-5 cm thick
Leaves: Ovate, 18-25 cm in diameter, sessile
Flowers: White, pink and purple, flowering in April-May
Fruit: Drupes, orange and red in colour

phytoconstituents: Roots and rhizomes: bergenin (C-glycoside), β-sitosterol, gallic acid, tannic acid

karma.karma: Diuretic, anti-inflammatory, haemostatic, cardiotonic, antitoxic | Other: अश्मरीभेदन, बस्तिशोधक, अर्शोघ्न, प्रमेहहर, शूलहर, व्रणहर, योनिरोगनाशक, कुष्ठघ्न, प्लीहारोगहर, बल्य, हृद्रोगहर

rogaghnata: अर्श, मूत्रकृच्छ्र, अश्मरी, प्रमेह, शूल, योनिरोग, प्लीहारोग, दाह, हृद्रोग, व्रण
amayika_prayoga: अश्मरी (calculus) व मूत्रकृच्छ्र (dysuria) = Milk boiled with Nala, Ashmabheda, Darbha, Ikshu, seeds of Trapusha and Ervaruka, taken with ghee

name: Patha
sloka: पाठापाठ्या कटूष्णा स्याद्वात श्लेष्महरी लघुः | हन्ति शूलज्वरातिसारकुष्ठहृद्रोगसूतीरुजः ||

synonyms.meaning: Seen in eastern part of India
Leaves have 3 or more prominent veins

classification_of_gana: चरक: संधानीय वर्ग, ज्वरहर वर्ग, स्तन्यशोधन वर्ग
सुश्रुत: आरग्वधादि गण, अम्बष्ठादि गण, पिप्पल्यादि गण, पटोलादि गण, गुडुच्यादि वर्ग

external_morphology.habitat: Seen mainly in northern India; Rajapatha more common in South India
details: Flowers: Male flowers pedicelled, seen in axillary cymes
Fruit: Drupe, compressed, red on ripening

phytoconstituents: Alkaloids like hayatine, hayatinine, hayatidine

karma.karma: ज्वरविनाशक, स्तन्यशोधन, शूलहर, गर्भाशयविशोधन, कुष्ठघ्न, जन्तुघ्न, बालग्रहाहारक, व्रणहर, भग्नसंधानकृत्

rogaghnata: ज्वर, स्तन्यदुष्टि, व्रण, शूल, बन्धकत्व, अतिसार, उदरशूल, कृमी, विषविकार, कण्डू, दाह, गलगण्ड
amayika_prayoga: प्रमेह (Prameha) = In Tarunameha, decoction of Patha and Aguru is beneficial
अतिसार (Diarrhoea) = For burning sensation in diarrhoea, Patha triturated with curd and given internally

name: Pippali
sloka: पिप्पली मागधी कृष्णा वैदेही चपला कणा | उपकुल्या शौण्डी कोल्या व्यालीक्ष्ण तण्डुला || गर्भश्याशूद्विकार बल्या च सुगन्धिकम् ||

synonyms.meaning: Red in colour on ripening but turns black once dried

classification_of_gana: चरक: कासहर वर्ग, कण्ठ्य वर्ग, हिक्कानिग्रहण वर्ग, पिप्पल्यादि वर्ग, तृष्णानिग्रहण वर्ग

external_morphology.habitat: Cultivated in Bengal, Bihar
details: Fruits: Small, ovoid, blackish green colour

phytoconstituents: Piperine, piplatine, n-hexadecane, n-heptadecane

karma.dosha_karma: कफवातशामक (Kaphavatashamak). Agryakarma: Root of Pippali - Appetizer, Digestant, Carminative
karma.karma: वमन, रसायन, वृष्य, केश्य, श्वासहर, कासहर, ज्वरहर, कुष्ठघ्न, प्रमेहहर, शूलनाशक

rogaghnata: Pippali: अग्निमांद्य, ज्वर, श्वास, कुष्ठ | Pippali Mula: गुल्म, श्वास | Simhali Pippali: अग्निमांद्य, हिक्का | Vanadi Pippali: अरुची
amayika_prayoga: शूल (Colic) = Combination of Pippali + Shunthi
कास (Cough) = Powder of Pippali + Kantakari with honey
ज्वर (Fever) = Decoction of Pippali with 4 times water
कफलीरोग = Decoction of Pippali mula + goat's urine
varieties: राजनिघण्टु: Pippali, Gajapippali, Simhali Pippali, Vanadi Pippali
